description: Global (self-hosted admin) config for the central Renovate runner — NOT this repo's own
dependency config (that stays in .github/renovate.json). Loaded by .github/workflows/
renovate.yml; see its header comment for the full auth/token-scope rationale.
customEnvVariables.BASE_REF arms the supply-chain tamper gate in the binary-checksum
postUpgradeTask fleet-wide: scripts/refresh-binary-checksums.sh only activates its gate
when BASE_REF is set, so it then re-pins a *_SHA256 only when the adjacent *_VERSION
actually changed vs BASE_REF and FAILS if a SHA changed under an unchanged version (a
swapped release asset on a fixed tag). origin/HEAD is base-branch-agnostic — it resolves
to each managed repo's default branch in Renovate's working clone, so `git show
origin/HEAD:<file>` reads the pre-upgrade version regardless of main/master. Renovate
injects customEnvVariables into the postUpgradeTask child env directly (no shell executor
needed); it is NOT in allowedEnv on purpose, so a managed repo's own `env` can't override
Fleet-wide automerge for non-major updates. platformAutomerge (default on) rides
GitHub native auto-merge, so a PR only lands once its required checks are green;
base.yaml sets allow_auto_merge fleet-wide and the shared ruleset requires CI.
Majors stay human-reviewed. A repo's own renovate.json packageRules can override.
